\subsection{play\_tone (\$7A7F)}

Generates a square wave tone by toggling the Apple~II speaker.
The outer loop runs 10~iterations (Y), each toggling the speaker
then delaying by X cycles.  After all 10~toggles, the whole sequence
repeats {\Tt{}SOUND{\textbackslash}{\_}DURATION\nwendquote} times.  {\Tt{}SOUND{\textbackslash}{\_}FREQ\nwendquote} controls the pitch
(higher value = lower frequency).

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{449}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}}}\moddef{play tone~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $7A7F
\nwlinkedidentc{PLAY_TONE}{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE

.outer:
    LDY     #$0A                    ; 10 speaker toggles per cycle
.toggle:
    LDA     $C030                   ; toggle speaker
    DEX
    BNE     .toggle                 ; inner delay loop
    LDX     \nwlinkedidentc{SOUND_FREQ}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}              ; reload pitch counter
    DEY
    BNE     .toggle                 ; next toggle
    DEC     \nwlinkedidentc{SOUND_DURATION}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}          ; repeat whole cycle
    BNE     .outer
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{PLAY{\_}TONE}}{PLAY:unTONE}{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{PLAY{\_}TONE}}{PLAY:unTONE}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}DURATION}}{SOUND:unDURATION}}\\{{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}FREQ}}{SOUND:unFREQ}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}DURATION}}{SOUND:unDURATION}{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}FREQ}}{SOUND:unFREQ}{PYNWmai0-DKDEJLHVFZFB5XCPIKJ6VYCXTM000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{450}\nwdocspar

\subsection{play\_noise (\$7A91)}

Generates noise by toggling the speaker while rotating a shift
register ({\Tt{}NOISE{\textbackslash}{\_}SHIFT\nwendquote}).  The ROL/ROR pair with the speaker read
creates pseudo-random bit patterns that produce a ``hiss'' or
explosion-like sound.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{451}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}}}\moddef{play noise~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $7A91
\nwlinkedidentc{PLAY_NOISE}{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE

.outer:
    LDY     #$0A                    ; 10 toggles per cycle
.toggle:
    LDA     $C030                   ; toggle speaker
    ROL     \nwlinkedidentc{NOISE_SHIFT}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}             ; rotate shift register left
    DEX
    BNE     .toggle                 ; inner delay loop
    LDX     \nwlinkedidentc{SOUND_FREQ}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}              ; reload pitch counter
    ROL     \nwlinkedidentc{NOISE_SHIFT}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}             ; \\ rotate left then right =
    ROR     \nwlinkedidentc{NOISE_SHIFT}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}             ; / feedback from carry bit
    NOP                             ; \\
    NOP                             ;  | timing padding
    NOP                             ; /
    DEY
    BNE     .toggle                 ; next toggle
    DEC     \nwlinkedidentc{SOUND_DURATION}{PYNWmai0-AJE4SNIWWSAT23GB2VNG3SS6JI000000-2}          ; repeat whole cycle
    BNE     .outer
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{PLAY{\_}NOISE}}{PLAY:unNOISE}{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{PLAY{\_}NOISE}}{PLAY:unNOISE}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{NOISE{\_}SHIFT}}{NOISE:unSHIFT}}\\{{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}DURATION}}{SOUND:unDURATION}}\\{{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}FREQ}}{SOUND:unFREQ}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{NOISE{\_}SHIFT}}{NOISE:unSHIFT}{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}DURATION}}{SOUND:unDURATION}{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SOUND{\_}FREQ}}{SOUND:unFREQ}{PYNWmai0-JOHWSGNPHOGV3JMFIHMJPZ66QM000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{452}\nwdocspar

\section{Disk I/O helpers}

\subsection{setup\_disk\_read (\$6C18)}

Prepares disk I/O parameters for the disk routines.  Stores
the track number (A) at offset~\$0C of the parameter block at~(\$08),
clears the sector offset at~(\$08)+3, then calls {\Tt{}RWTS{\textbackslash}{\_}ENTRY\nwendquote}
at~{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}B7B5\nwendquote} with the IOB address from~\$08/\$09.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{453}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}}}\moddef{setup disk read~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $6C18
\nwlinkedidentc{SETUP_DISK_READ}{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE

    LDY     #$0C
    STA     ($08),Y                 ; ($08)+$0C = track number (A)
    LDA     #$00
    LDY     #$03
    STA     ($08),Y                 ; ($08)+$03 = 0 (sector offset)
    LDA     $09                     ; \\ A/Y = slot parameter block
    LDY     $08                     ; /
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{RWTS_ENTRY}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-31}              ; seek to track
    LDA     #$00
    STA     $48                     ; clear disk status
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{SETUP{\_}DISK{\_}READ}}{SETUP:unDISK:unREAD}{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{SETUP{\_}DISK{\_}READ}}{SETUP:unDISK:unREAD}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{RWTS{\_}ENTRY}}{RWTS:unENTRY}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{RWTS{\_}ENTRY}}{RWTS:unENTRY}{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{454}\nwdocspar

\subsection{load\_scene\_data (\$6DD8)}

Loads scene-specific data from disk into the {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}5C00\nwendquote} region.  The disk
read calls {\Tt{}DISK{\textbackslash}{\_}READ{\textbackslash}{\_}FIELD\nwendquote} at {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}B300\nwendquote}, which enters the disk~VM
interpreter to perform the multi-sector read.  If the read fails, the
routine fills a byte with PRNG output (a graceful degradation rather than
crashing).

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{455}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}}}\moddef{load scene data~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $6DD8
\nwlinkedidentc{LOAD_SCENE_DATA}{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE

    BIT     $C0E9                   ; check disk controller
    LDA     #$05                    ; \\
    LDY     #$04                    ;  | ($08)+4 = 5 (track configuration)
    STA     ($08),Y                 ; /
    LDA     #$00
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{SETUP_DISK_READ}{PYNWmai0-3U33XUD5TOMNWRAM7IHRXHS7HA000000-1}         ; prepare disk parameters
    CLC
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_READ_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}         ; read sector data from disk
    BCS     .error
    RTS

.error:
    LDY     \nwlinkedidentc{PRNG_OUTPUT}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-11}             ; on error: fill with PRNG data
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{STEP_PRNG}{PYNWmai0-3OGDZ4EYEYE7YXQHNEV2CZPRBM000000-1}
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{PRNG_OUTPUT}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-11}
    STA     $9AA6,Y
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{LOAD{\_}SCENE{\_}DATA}}{LOAD:unSCENE:unDATA}{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{LOAD{\_}SCENE{\_}DATA}}{LOAD:unSCENE:unDATA}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}READ{\_}FIELD}}{DISK:unREAD:unFIELD}}\\{{\nwixident{PRNG{\_}OUTPUT}}{PRNG:unOUTPUT}}\\{{\nwixident{SETUP{\_}DISK{\_}READ}}{SETUP:unDISK:unREAD}}\\{{\nwixident{STEP{\_}PRNG}}{STEP:unPRNG}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}READ{\_}FIELD}}{DISK:unREAD:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{PRNG{\_}OUTPUT}}{PRNG:unOUTPUT}{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SETUP{\_}DISK{\_}READ}}{SETUP:unDISK:unREAD}{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{STEP{\_}PRNG}}{STEP:unPRNG}{PYNWmai0-3XMQPOG4ARI455O23BBDT5GCTE000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{456}\nwdocspar

\section{Unresolved: \$A478}
\label{sec:a478}

The scene loop calls {\Tt{}JSR\ {\textbackslash}{\$}A478\nwendquote} every iteration to check for user
input.  This address is in the EALDR region ({\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}A000\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}BFFF\nwendquote}),
which remains in memory after the main program starts.

The bytes at {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}A478\nwendquote} include the undocumented NMOS 6502 opcode {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}04\nwendquote}
(a 2-byte NOP).  This could mean the code legitimately uses undocumented
instructions, or that the region is modified at runtime before being called.

Partial decode:

\begin{verbatim}
    A478: 04 E5         ; NOP $E5 (DOP: 2-byte NOP, reads zp $E5)
    A47A: 19 03 64      ; ORA $6403,Y
    ...
    A483: CA            ; DEX
    A484: D0 FD         ; BNE $A483   (inner delay loop)
    A486: 38            ; SEC
    A487: E9 01         ; SBC #$01
    A489: D0 F6         ; BNE $A481   (outer delay loop)
    A48B: 60            ; RTS
\end{verbatim}

The inner loop at {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}A483\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}A484\nwendquote} ({\Tt{}DEX;\ BNE\nwendquote}) is a standard
delay loop.  The outer loop decrements A and repeats, making this a
nested delay.  The carry flag state on exit likely indicates whether a
key was pressed (C=1) or the timer expired (C=0).  Further analysis
requires an emulator that supports undocumented 6502 opcodes.

\section{Summary}

The attract screen call flow:

\begin{itemize}
  \item {\Tt{}MAIN{\textbackslash}{\_}ENTRY\nwendquote} ({\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}0800\nwendquote}): calls {\Tt{}GAME{\textbackslash}{\_}INIT\nwendquote}, then
        {\Tt{}ATTRACT{\textbackslash}{\_}SCREEN\nwendquote} if {\Tt{}TOTAL{\textbackslash}{\_}MOB{\textbackslash}{\_}COUNT\nwendquote} is zero.
  \item {\Tt{}ATTRACT{\textbackslash}{\_}SCREEN\nwendquote} ({\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}09F4\nwendquote}): sets scene index 1, calls
        {\Tt{}COMPUTE{\textbackslash}{\_}SCENE{\textbackslash}{\_}PTR\nwendquote}, tail-calls {\Tt{}SCENE{\textbackslash}{\_}LOOP\nwendquote} with scene \$26.
  \item {\Tt{}SCENE{\textbackslash}{\_}LOOP\nwendquote} ({\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}6C37\nwendquote}): saves ZP pointers, steps PRNG,
        conditionally loads scene data from disk.  In a loop: checks
        keyboard/timer via {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}A478\nwendquote}, displays status bar, renders scene
        text via {\Tt{}TEXT{\textbackslash}{\_}RENDERER\nwendquote}, runs {\Tt{}SCRIPT{\textbackslash}{\_}ENGINE\nwendquote} with script \#3.
        Exits when user presses a key or scene sequence ends.
\end{itemize}

Key subsystems:
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Text renderer} ({\Tt{}TEXT{\textbackslash}{\_}RENDERER\nwendquote}): Reads byte streams with
        position commands, pause/reset control codes, and character data
        for the HRCG font.
  \item \textbf{Script engine} ({\Tt{}SCRIPT{\textbackslash}{\_}ENGINE\nwendquote}): Runs short bytecode
        programs from {\Tt{}SCRIPT{\textbackslash}{\_}TABLE\nwendquote}.  Supports loops, gosub, action
        dispatch with PRNG randomization.
  \item \textbf{Resident interpreter} ({\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}0500\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}07FF\nwendquote}): A
        secondary VM copied from EALDR, using context-swapped zero-page
        state and XOR-encrypted addresses.
  \item \textbf{PRNG} ({\Tt{}STEP{\textbackslash}{\_}PRNG\nwendquote}): Linear congruential generator with
        formula $\texttt{val}_{n+1} = 69 \cdot \texttt{val}_n + \$53
        \pmod{256}$.
\end{itemize}


\chapter{Animating the splash screen}

The {\Tt{}start{\_}splash{\_}screen\nwendquote} routine first loads up the initial splash screen,
sets {\Tt{}indirect{\_}routine\nwendquote} to {\Tt{}note{\_}played\nwendquote}, and begins the sound routine by
calling {\Tt{}start{\_}sound\nwendquote}.

{\Tt{}note{\_}played\nwendquote} plots a mob, then sets {\Tt{}indirect{\_}routine\nwendquote} to {\Tt{}splash{\_}routine{\_}2\nwendquote}
and jumps to {\Tt{}next{\_}table{\_}entry\nwendquote}.

{\Tt{}splash{\_}routine{\_}2\nwendquote} calls a routine first with {\Tt{}{\$}5E1F\nwendquote} then with {\Tt{}{\$}5E3A\nwendquote},
then jumps to {\Tt{}splash{\_}screen{\_}step\nwendquote}.

{\Tt{}splash{\_}screen{\_}step\nwendquote} then sets {\Tt{}indirect{\_}routine\nwendquote} to {\Tt{}splash{\_}routine{\_}1\nwendquote}
then {\Tt{}splash{\_}routine{\_}0\nwendquote} and calls {\Tt{}set{\_}splash{\_}routine\nwendquote} within {\Tt{}note{\_}played\nwendquote}.
After that, {\Tt{}splash{\_}screen{\_}step\nwendquote} presumably moves mobs around.


When the user ends the splash screen, the {\Tt{}start{\_}splash{\_}screen\nwendquote} routine
replaces the indirect jump to {\Tt{}indirect{\_}routine\nwendquote} in {\Tt{}start{\_}sound\nwendquote} to an
absolute jump to {\Tt{}next{\_}table{\_}entry\nwendquote}, since there is no further need to
animate while playing sound.


\chapter{The data}
\subsection{Message data}

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{457}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-MA3WDYAYUKINVQ6W4447HHOCBE000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-MA3WDYAYUKINVQ6W4447HHOCBE000000-1}}}\moddef{Message data~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-MA3WDYAYUKINVQ6W4447HHOCBE000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwenddeflinemarkup
\nwlinkedidentc{MESSAGE_DATA}{PYNWmai0-MA3WDYAYUKINVQ6W4447HHOCBE000000-1}:
    APSTR ""
    APSTR "GOLD EATING ROCK SLIME GOBBLES THE CASH."
    APSTR ""
    APSTR "YOUR USED ARMOR IS WORTHLESS."
    APSTR ""
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{MESSAGE{\_}DATA}}{MESSAGE:unDATA}{PYNWmai0-MA3WDYAYUKINVQ6W4447HHOCBE000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{MESSAGE{\_}DATA}}{MESSAGE:unDATA}}}\nwnotused{Message data}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{458}\nwdocspar

\subsection{Location data}

Location data and character data share the same 9-byte record table at
{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}4000\nwendquote}.  {\Tt{}GET{\textbackslash}{\_}CHAR{\textbackslash}{\_}RECORD\nwendquote} indexes into this table (index~$\times$~9
+ \$4000).  Record~0 at \$4000 is a header; records~1--62 are the actual
entries.  {\Tt{}LOCATION{\textbackslash}{\_}DATA\nwendquote} points to record~1 (\$4009).

Each record is 9~bytes:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{cll}
\textbf{Offset} & \textbf{Name}     & \textbf{Description} \\
\hline
0--1 & name\_ptr   & 16-bit pointer to length-prefixed location name \\
2    & mob\_head   & Head of mob linked list (character index, 0~=~none) \\
3    & min\_pos    & Top-left bounding box (row $\times$ 20 + col) \\
4    & max\_pos    & Bottom-right bounding box (row $\times$ 20 + col) \\
5    & map\_id     & Bits 0--6: map scene ID.  Bit~7: visited flag (set at runtime) \\
6--7 & event\_ptr  & 16-bit pointer to event/object list (3-byte entries) \\
8    & mob\_count  & Number of mobs (summed into {\Tt{}TOTAL{\textbackslash}{\_}MOB{\textbackslash}{\_}COUNT\nwendquote})
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

The event list at {\Tt{}event{\textbackslash}{\_}ptr\nwendquote} consists of 3-byte entries (see
\S\ref{sec:event-format} for the full format).  Byte~0 is a map position
(\$FE~=~empty slot, \$FF~=~end-of-list); byte~1 encodes the event type
and status; byte~2 is type-dependent action data.

The map scene~ID (field~5, bits~0--6) is compared with {\Tt{}LOCATION{\textbackslash}{\_}FLAG2\nwendquote}
(\$5A56); when it changes, {\Tt{}FILL{\textbackslash}{\_}MAP\nwendquote} is called to redraw the map.
Bit~7 is set on first entry via {\Tt{}ORA\ {\textbackslash}{\#}{\textbackslash}{\$}80\nwendquote} and tested with {\Tt{}BMI\nwendquote} to
skip first-visit logic on subsequent entries.

The sentinel {\Tt{}FFFF\nwendquote} in the name pointer marks the end of the table.

\subsection{Event list format}
\label{sec:event-format}

Each location has a variable-length event list pointed to by
{\Tt{}event{\textbackslash}{\_}ptr\nwendquote} (location record fields~6--7).  Entries are 3~bytes each,
terminated by {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}FF\nwendquote} followed by two {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}FF\nwendquote} padding bytes.  Each
location's first entry is normally an empty slot ({\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}FE\nwendquote}) reserved for
runtime use (e.g.\ dropped treasure after combat).

\paragraph{Byte~0: map position.}
A value 0--199 encodes a grid position (row~$\times$~20~+~col).
{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}FE\nwendquote} marks an empty slot that can be filled at runtime.
{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}FF\nwendquote} marks the end of the list.

\paragraph{Byte~1: event type.}
The top two bits select the category:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\textbf{Byte~1 range} & \textbf{Category} & \textbf{Details} \\
\hline
\$00--\$3F & Random encounter & Probability check vs.\ character stat; \\
           &                  & on fail, bit~6 is set $\to$ becomes specific encounter \\
\$40--\$BF & Specific encounter & bits~5--0 = mob index $\to$ {\Tt{}ACTIVE{\textbackslash}{\_}CHAR\nwendquote} \\
\$C0--\$D1 & Dropped treasure & gold = ((\texttt{byte1} $-$ \$C0) $\times$ 256) + \texttt{byte2} \\
\$D2       & Persistent scene & \texttt{byte2} = scene ID; not cleared after trigger \\
\$D3       & One-time scene   & \texttt{byte2} = scene ID; slot cleared to \$FE \\
\$D4       & Scene + item drop & like \$D3 but also spawns 1--4 bonus items \\
\$D5--\$DA & Map/area event   & indexed from table at \$7EDD \\
\$DB--\$FD & (unknown)        & \\
\$FE       & Empty-slot data  & \texttt{byte2} has sub-encoding
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\paragraph{Byte~2: action data} (meaning depends on byte~1).
For encounters (\$40--\$BF), this is the map position where the mob
appears.  For treasure (\$C0--\$D1), the gold amount low byte.
For scene events (\$D2--\$D4), a scene/event~ID.

\paragraph{Key routines.}
\begin{itemize}
\item \$0B62 --- searches a position for mobs first, then events;
      returns {\Tt{}ENTITY{\textbackslash}{\_}REC\nwendquote} pointing to the found entry.
\item {\Tt{}FUN{\textbackslash}{\_}138D\nwendquote} (\$138D) --- finds the nearest non-activated event
      (byte~1~$<$~\$C0) using Manhattan distance; used for AI
      pathfinding.
\item \$1260 --- fills empty {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}FE\nwendquote} slots with combat loot, or
      accumulates value in \$C0--\$D0 entries (treasure drops).
      Entries saturate at byte~1~=~\$D1.
\item {\Tt{}HANDLE{\textbackslash}{\_}ENCOUNTER\nwendquote} (\$1C01) --- encounter handler: dispatches
      random vs.\ specific encounters based on byte~1~$<$~\$40.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Mob data and name strings}

Immediately after the event lists, the region {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}4C80--{\textbackslash}{\$}5AFF\nwendquote} holds
the mob data table and associated name strings.

\begin{itemize}
\item {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}4C80--{\textbackslash}{\$}547F\nwendquote}: 128 mob data records, 16~bytes each.  Bytes
  0--1 are a pointer to the mob's name string; the remaining 14~bytes
  encode combat stats, group linkage, position, appearance, and level.
  {\Tt{}GET{\textbackslash}{\_}MOB{\textbackslash}{\_}DATA\nwendquote} indexes into this table via the base pointer
  at~{\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}4000/{\textbackslash}{\$}4001\nwendquote}.
\item {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}5480--{\textbackslash}{\$}59C8\nwendquote}: Length-prefixed high-bit ASCII name strings
  for all 128 mobs (``ALI BABA'', ``SCHEHERAZADE'', ``MINOTAUR'',~etc.).
\item {\Tt{}{\textbackslash}{\$}59C9--{\textbackslash}{\$}5AFF\nwendquote}: Residual debug/diagnostic text (unused at
  runtime).
\end{itemize}
